Container.Name: StandardNotes-Server
Container.Overview:
Standard Notes – self-hosted backend for Unraid (community template). End-to-end encrypted notes, sync server, files server, auth and API gateway bundled into a single image (standardnotes/server) maintained upstream.
IMPORTANT: This template installs ONLY the Standard Notes server image. You must run a MariaDB and a Redis container SEPARATELY before starting this container. See the project README for the recommended Unraid templates and required DB/Redis settings.
Required, in this order: 1. MariaDB container (for example MariaDB-Official from CA), empty database "standard_notes_db" with user "std_notes_user". 2. Redis container (Redis-Official from CA, default port 6379). 3. (Optional) The companion StandardNotes-LocalStack template, which provides the SNS/SQS endpoints expected by the upstream image. 4. This template – Standard Notes server.
Pre-flight: generate THREE 32-byte hex secrets and paste them into the fields below. On any Linux/Mac shell: openssl rand -hex 32 # repeat 3x
What's pre-configured in this template: • Server port 3000/tcp (HTTP API gateway, put behind your reverse proxy) • Files port 3125 host -> 3104 container (uploads / files server) • Persistent volumes for /var/lib/server/logs and the uploads directory • DB_TYPE=mysql (internal driver value required for MariaDB — Standard Notes / TypeORM uses the "mysql" driver string to talk to MariaDB), CACHE_TYPE=redis (per upstream .env.sample) • All env variables exposed as Unraid fields, with safe defaults
Notes: • This is an UNOFFICIAL community template. Standard Notes does not provide support for self-hosted setups beyond the official Docker docs at https://standardnotes.com/help/self-hosting/docker. • The web client (standardnotes/web) is optional and is NOT part of this template. Most users connect with the official desktop / mobile apps and just point them at https://your-domain/ via reverse proxy. • Always expose Standard Notes via HTTPS (SWAG, Nginx Proxy Manager, Traefik, Caddy, …). Do not publish port 3000 to the public internet directly.
SYNC-LOOP / DUPLICATE-NOTES WARNING: Misconfiguration of Redis, COOKIE_DOMAIN, the reverse proxy, or the image tag can trigger massive note duplication (a single new note replicating dozens of times). ALWAYS test with a fresh throwaway account and one client only before migrating real notes. If duplication starts, stop all clients, stop this container, and inspect the logs/database before continuing. See the project README section "Sync-Loop / Duplicate Notes Guardrails" and docs/sync-loop-troubleshooting.md for the full checklist.
Image tag (Repository field above): defaults to standardnotes/server:latest. To pin a known-good release, change the tag in the Repository field, e.g. standardnotes/server:1.32.0. Change tags carefully — downgrading across schema migrations can leave the database unreadable to the older image.
Container.Requires: A separately running MariaDB container and a Redis container reachable from this container's network (bridge or routable LAN). See the project README for details.

Config.Name: API / Gateway Port
Config.Description: HTTP port of the Standard Notes API gateway. Put this behind a reverse proxy with HTTPS termination — do NOT expose directly to the public internet.

Config.Name: Files Server Port
Config.Description: Files server port. Upstream maps host 3125 → container 3104. Keep this default unless you know why you are changing it.

Config.Name: Uploads (files server)
Config.Description: Persistent storage for the encrypted file uploads served by the files server.

Config.Name: DB Host (DB_HOST)
Config.Description: REQUIRED. Hostname or IP of your MariaDB container. When MariaDB and Standard Notes share the same Unraid host on a custom Docker bridge network, STRONGLY prefer the Unraid container name (e.g. 'mariadb') — IPs of bridge containers change on restart. When MariaDB lives on a different host / VLAN (e.g. 192.168.20.71), a static LAN IP is fine. Either way the address must be reachable from this container.

Config.Name: DB Port (DB_PORT)
Config.Description: Port of your MariaDB server. Default 3306.

Config.Name: DB Username (DB_USERNAME)
Config.Description: Database user. Must match the user you created in MariaDB.

Config.Name: DB Name (DB_DATABASE)
Config.Description: Database name. Must already exist (empty is fine — migrations run automatically on first start).

Config.Name: DB Type (DB_TYPE)
Config.Description: Database driver. Leave at 'mysql'. This is an INTERNAL DRIVER VALUE REQUIRED FOR MARIADB — Standard Notes / TypeORM uses the 'mysql' driver string to talk to MariaDB. Do not change this; the templates and docs assume MariaDB.

Config.Name: Redis Host (REDIS_HOST)
Config.Description: REQUIRED. Hostname or IP of your Redis container. When Redis and Standard Notes share the same Unraid host on a custom Docker bridge network, STRONGLY prefer the Unraid container name (e.g. 'redis') — bridge IPs change on restart. When Redis lives on a different host / VLAN (e.g. 192.168.20.72), a static LAN IP is fine. Test reachability from this container with: nc -zv <host> 6379. If unreachable, the server logs ECONNREFUSED and sync state can de-duplicate incorrectly across requests, which has been linked to runaway note duplication. See docs/sync-loop-troubleshooting.md.

Config.Name: Redis Port (REDIS_PORT)
Config.Description: REQUIRED. Port of your Redis server. Default 6379. Must match what the Redis container actually listens on. Test with: nc -zv <redis-host> 6379 from inside this container.

Config.Name: JWT Secret (AUTH_JWT_SECRET)
Config.Description: REQUIRED. 32-byte hex secret. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32. Changing this invalidates all existing sessions.

Config.Name: Auth Server Encryption Key (AUTH_SERVER_ENCRYPTION_SERVER_KEY)
Config.Description: REQUIRED. 32-byte hex secret. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32. Encrypts data at rest on the server side. Losing this means losing access to data — back it up.

Config.Name: Valet Token Secret (VALET_TOKEN_SECRET)
Config.Description: REQUIRED. 32-byte hex secret. Generate with: openssl rand -hex 32. Signs short-lived upload/download tokens for the files server.

Config.Name: Public Files Server URL (PUBLIC_FILES_SERVER_URL)
Config.Description: Optional. The publicly reachable HTTPS URL of the files server, e.g. https://files.standardnotes.bottich.lol. Set this ONLY if you reverse-proxy the files server on its own hostname/subdomain (recommended for attachment support). Leave empty if you do not need attachments — note creation, editing, and sync work without it. Single-domain path proxying (one host, /files/* prefix) is not reliably supported by the upstream image.

Config.Name: Cookie Domain (COOKIE_DOMAIN)
Config.Description: Optional but STRONGLY RECOMMENDED behind a reverse proxy. Must match the public sync host (e.g. standardnotes.bottich.lol). HTTPS is required for Secure cookies outside localhost. A wrong or empty value here can cause "No cookies provided for cookie-based session token" in the server log and trigger a /v1/items session loop, which has been linked to runaway note duplication. Leave empty only if you access the server directly on localhost. See docs/sync-loop-troubleshooting.md.
